page: OVHcloud b3-64 is a general-purpose virtual server instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 16 dedicated physical cores on an x86_64 AMD EPYC Milan processor running at 2.3 GHz, with one thread per core. The hardware configuration includes 64.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. Storage is provided via a 400 GB local NVMe SSD, and network connectivity is rated at a 4 Gbps baseline.

Benchmark results indicate strong single-core performance, with stress-ng single-core and Geekbench single-core scores placing in the top 25 percent of tested servers. Large-block memory bandwidth and Passmark memory scores are also in the top tier, indicating efficient data transfer for larger datasets. However, multi-core performance metrics, such as stress-ng multi-core and Geekbench multi-core scores, fall into the average tier. Cryptographic and compression tasks, including bzip3 decompression, show strong throughput, while database operations and Redis SET benchmarks perform at average levels.

The dedicated core allocation ensures predictable CPU performance without resource sharing, which represents a solid value for workloads requiring consistent compute power. However, the lack of hyperthreading and average multi-core scaling are notable tradeoffs for highly parallelized applications. The 400 GB local NVMe SSD provides fast local storage, making this instance suitable for general-purpose workloads, text processing, software compilation, and moderate database hosting.
description: The OVHcloud b3-64 is a general-purpose virtual server built on the KVM hypervisor and powered by an x86_64 AMD EPYC Milan processor. It features 16 dedicated physical cores running at 2.3 GHz with one thread per core, paired with 64.0 GB of RAM and a 400 GB local NVMe SSD. Network performance is supported by a 4 Gbps baseline bandwidth. Benchmarks reveal strong single-core CPU and large-block memory bandwidth performance, placing in the top 25 percent, while multi-core execution and database operations perform in the average tier. The dedicated CPU allocation provides predictable performance without resource contention, though the lack of hyperthreading limits highly parallel scaling. This configuration is suited for text processing, software compilation, and general-purpose application hosting.
og_description: OVHcloud b3-64 is a general-purpose virtual server featuring 16 dedicated AMD EPYC Milan cores, 64 GB RAM, and a 400 GB NVMe SSD, delivering strong single-core and memory bandwidth performance.
meta_description: OVHcloud b3-64 is a general-purpose KVM instance with 16 AMD EPYC Milan cores, 64 GB RAM, and a 400 GB NVMe SSD for predictable workloads.
tagline: A dedicated AMD EPYC instance with balanced memory and local NVMe storage for predictable single-core and data-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: 16 dedicated AMD EPYC Milan physical cores with no hyperthreading
64 GB of system memory providing 4 GB per core
400 GB of local NVMe SSD storage for low-latency data access
Strong single-core CPU and large-block memory bandwidth benchmarks
4 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with one public IPv4 address
Suited for text processing, software compilation, and general-purpose hosting
